title: Workflow manifest
description: A lightweight, machine-readable lifecycle: ordered steps, the gates that authorize each step, allowed transitions, and stop conditions. Prose belongs in systems/ docs, not here.

id.description: Lowercase, hyphen-separated workflow id. Must match the file name stem and the registry entry.

steps.description: Ordered, unique step ids. The lifecycle advances through these in order, subject to gates.

gates.description: Map of step id -> the conditions that must hold before that step may start. Keys must be steps. Conditions are free-form tokens (e.g. 'approved-spec', 'test-evidence') checked by the agent or a validator.

stopConditions.description: Workflow-local stop conditions, in addition to the global rules/stop-conditions.md.

validation.description: Optional map of step id -> a deterministic command that verifies the step's artifacts.

artifacts.description: Optional map of step id -> the artifact paths (relative to a spec folder) the step is expected to produce.
